catalyst.event: Geely Automobile Holdings 2026 H1 interim results — consolidated revenue, GM, OpM, net income disclosure; segment disclosure granularity to be assessed
catalyst.date_or_window: August 2026 (exact reporting date not in our data; inferred from HK interim cadence)
catalyst.source: Standard HK interim reporting cadence; exact issuer calendar not available in our data

rightIf_conditions: N/A — PASS; no tradeable direction asserted | source: N/A | if_not_disclosed: N/A
wrongIf_conditions: N/A — PASS; no tradeable direction asserted | source: N/A | if_not_disclosed: N/A
what_changes_our_mind: H1 2026 interim report discloses segment-level GM/OpM by geography (overseas vs domestic) OR by brand cluster (Geely/ZEEKR/Lotus/Polestar) — at that point the mix-margin mechanism becomes measurable and the thesis becomes tradeable.
variant_view_one_sentence: Market believes Geely's global brand mix + ZEEKR consolidation will drive operating leverage → We CANNOT confirm or refute this because segment economics are not disclosed → PASS on INSUFFICIENT_DATA until segment-level GM/OpM prints or a primary business-mechanism signal (broker channel check, mgmt segment commentary) becomes available.

core_causal_link.link_sentence: Brand/geography/powertrain mix shift drives consolidated margin expansion at H1 2026.
core_causal_link.tier_justification: SEGMENT ECONOMICS explicitly not_available (no_segment_disclosure). Per RULE X, peer-relative revenue growth (+15.6% vs BYD -11.8%) is an [E1] fact but is NOT a valid [E1] structural base because the forward mechanism is segment/mix-driven and the issuer does not disclose segment economics — so the H1 consolidated print cannot uniquely re-measure the causal claim. To make this [E1]: need segment-level GM by geography (overseas vs domestic) or by brand cluster disclosed in the H1 interim report, OR a primary-source broker channel check / institutional visit note with segment margin commentary.

pass_reason_detail: SEGMENT ECONOMICS = not_available (no_segment_disclosure). The Bull's variant mechanism (Lotus hybrid pivot + ZEEKR consolidation + global premium mix → margin accretion / operating leverage) is segment/mix-driven, but Geely does not disclose segment-level GM/OpM by brand (Lotus/Polestar/Volvo/ZEEKR/core Geely) or by geography (overseas vs domestic). At the H1 2026 catalyst, only consolidated GM/OpM will print — that is consistent with multiple incompatible causal stories (mix improvement, domestic price-war easing, FX, raw-material cost) and cannot uniquely confirm or refute the variant. Per RULE X, peer-relative revenue growth (+15.6% vs BYD -11.8%) is an [E1] fact but is NOT a valid [E1] structural base for this forward mix-margin claim because the causal mechanism is unmeasurable in issuer disclosure. Needed to re-classify as tradeable: segment GM disclosure by geography or by brand sub-portfolio in H1 2026 interim filing.
